structure.affirmative: Subject + was/were + V-ing — She was reading. / They were playing.
structure.negative: Subject + wasn't/weren't + V-ing — She wasn't reading. / They weren't playing.
structure.question: Was/Were + Subject + V-ing? — Was she reading? / Were they playing?

usage.text: Action in progress at a specific past moment
Background action interrupted by Past Simple
Setting the scene in a story or narrative

faq.a: Use 'was' with I / He / She / It. Use 'were' with You / We / They: 'She was sleeping' / 'They were sleeping'.

faq.q: What's the difference between Past Simple and Past Continuous?
faq.a: Past Simple = completed action ('She read the book'). Past Continuous = in-progress action ('She was reading when I arrived'). Past Continuous provides the background scene.

faq.q: Can Past Continuous stand alone without Past Simple?
faq.a: Yes: 'At midnight I was still working' — no interruption needed. It describes the ongoing state at a past moment.
